arrival.title: You've Arrived at the Polling Booth
arrival.description: It's election day. You've reached your assigned polling station. There's a queue outside and election officers are managing the crowd.

choices.label: Join the queue
I don't know which booth is mine
I'm nervous, what do I do?

branch_wrong_booth.title: Finding Your Correct Booth
branch_wrong_booth.description: Every voter is assigned to a specific booth based on your address. You can find yours by checking your EPIC card, or asking the Booth Level Officer (BLO) present outside.
branch_wrong_booth.tip: Your booth number is printed on your Voter ID. You can also check on the Voter Helpline app or call 1950.

choices.label: Found it, joining the queue now

branch_nervous.title: It's Okay to Be Nervous
branch_nervous.description: First-time voting feels overwhelming but the process is simple. Officers are there to help, not judge. You have every right to ask for assistance.
branch_nervous.tip: If you need help understanding the machine, you can ask the Presiding Officer inside — they're required to assist you.

choices.label: Okay I'm ready, joining the queue

identity_check.title: Identity Verification
identity_check.description: You're at the front of the queue. The polling officer asks for your Voter ID (EPIC card) to verify your name on the electoral roll.

choices.label: I have my Voter ID
I forgot my Voter ID
My name isn't on the list

branch_no_epic.title: No Voter ID? You May Still Vote
branch_no_epic.description: ECI allows 12 alternative photo ID documents if you don't have your EPIC card. These include Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, PAN card, MNREGA job card, and more.
branch_no_epic.tip: You cannot vote without any photo ID at all. But Voter ID is not the only option.

choices.label: I have an alternative ID
I have no ID at all

branch_no_id_at_all.title: Unfortunately You Cannot Vote Today
branch_no_id_at_all.description: Without any valid photo ID, the polling officer cannot verify your identity and you will not be permitted to cast your vote. This is to protect the integrity of the election.
branch_no_id_at_all.tip: For future elections, apply for your EPIC card at voterportal.eci.gov.in well in advance.

choices.label: Start over

branch_name_missing.title: Your Name Isn't on the List
branch_name_missing.description: If your name doesn't appear in the electoral roll at that booth, you cannot vote there even with valid ID. This could happen if you recently moved or your registration wasn't processed.
branch_name_missing.tip: You can file a complaint with the Returning Officer. For future elections, always verify your name at electoralsearch.eci.gov.in before polling day.

choices.label: Start over

name_found.title: Name Verified ✓
name_found.description: The officer finds your name on the electoral roll, marks it, and asks you to proceed to the next table for finger inking.

choices.label: Proceed to inking

inking.title: Finger Inking
inking.description: An officer applies indelible ink on the index finger of your left hand. This ink cannot be washed off for several days and proves you've already voted — preventing double voting.

choices.label: Done, what's next?
I already have ink on my finger

branch_already_inked.title: Already Inked Means Already Voted
branch_already_inked.description: If you already have indelible ink on your finger, it means a vote has already been cast in your name. This is a serious issue and should be reported immediately to the Presiding Officer.
branch_already_inked.tip: This could indicate voter fraud. You have the right to file a complaint. Contact the Election Commission helpline at 1950.

choices.label: Start over

enter_booth.title: Entering the Voting Compartment
enter_booth.description: You're handed a voter slip and directed to the voting compartment — a small private space with the EVM (Electronic Voting Machine). No phones allowed inside.

choices.label: Enter the compartment
Can I take my phone inside?

branch_phone.title: No Phones Inside the Booth
branch_phone.description: You are not allowed to carry your phone into the voting compartment. This prevents photographing the ballot which could compromise the secrecy of your vote.
branch_phone.tip: Leave your phone with a trusted person outside or in your pocket — just don't take it into the compartment.

choices.label: Understood, entering now

cast_vote.title: Casting Your Vote
cast_vote.description: The EVM shows a list of candidates with their party symbols. Press the button next to the candidate you want to vote for. The machine beeps and a light confirms your vote.

choices.label: I pressed the button and heard a beep
I'm confused by the machine
I don't want to vote for anyone

branch_confused_evm.title: Confused by the EVM?
branch_confused_evm.description: The EVM is simple — find your candidate's name and party symbol, then press the blue button next to it. If you're still confused, step out and ask the Presiding Officer for a demonstration. They must help you.
branch_confused_evm.tip: You cannot accidentally vote for the wrong person — each button only activates one candidate.

choices.label: Got it, casting my vote now

branch_nota.title: You Can Vote NOTA
branch_nota.description: NOTA stands for None of the Above. It's the last option on every EVM. If you don't want to vote for any candidate, you can press NOTA — your vote still counts as participation.
branch_nota.tip: NOTA votes are counted but do not affect the winner. The candidate with the most votes wins regardless.

choices.label: I'll press NOTA
Actually I'll vote for a candidate

vvpat.title: VVPAT Slip Appears
vvpat.description: After pressing the button, a paper slip appears in the VVPAT (Voter Verified Paper Audit Trail) machine for 7 seconds showing the candidate name and symbol you voted for. This is your proof.

choices.label: I saw the slip, vote confirmed
The slip shows the wrong candidate

branch_wrong_slip.title: Wrong Candidate on VVPAT?
branch_wrong_slip.description: If the VVPAT slip shows a different candidate than the one you pressed, immediately call the Presiding Officer without leaving the booth. This is a serious EVM malfunction and must be reported on the spot.
branch_wrong_slip.tip: Do not leave the booth before reporting this. Once you exit, the complaint may not be accepted.

choices.label: I've reported it to the officer

exit.title: You've Successfully Voted! 🎉
exit.description: You exit the booth. Your vote has been cast and counted. The ink on your finger is your badge of participation in Indian democracy.
exit.tip: Results are typically declared within 24-48 hours of polling day closing. You can follow live counts on ECI's official website.

choices.label: Try a different scenario
